zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergne於1410年出生在法國豪-諾門迪艾時，她的父親Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IV18歲，母親Jacquette DU PESCHIN14歲。她於1450年與Guillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnes結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。她去世於1488年9月8日在法國布格尼艾福可斯-庫斯-素-洛易多享年78歲，埋葬在法國崔傑。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergne于1410年出生在法国豪-諾門迪艾时，她的父亲Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IV18岁，母亲Jacquette DU PESCHIN14岁。她于1450年与Guillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnes结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。她去世于1488年9月8日在法国布格尼艾福可斯-庫斯-素-洛易多享年78岁，埋葬在法国崔傑。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Tour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ramez, Ashraf, Bakr, Mohamed, Mostafa, Nabil, Samir, Abdalla, Adib, Ali, Amir, Ebrahim.  Romanian Sorin, Dragos.
Romanian: nickname from nour, a variant of nor ‘cloud’.

variants.name: Nur
meanings: Muslim: from a personal name based on Arabic nūr ‘light, illumination’. Al-Nūr ‘the Light’ is an attribute of Allah, giving rise to the compound name ʿAbd al-Nūr, ʿAbdul-Nūr (see Abdelnour ). The word nūr is also found in combinations such as Nūr-Allāh or Nurullāh ‘Light of Allah’, Nūr ud-Dīn or Nuruddin ‘Light of Religion’. Compare Noor , Nor , and Nour .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergne was born in 1410, in Eure, Upper Normandy, France, her father, Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IV, was 18 and her mother, Jacquette DU PESCHIN, was 14. She married Guillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnes in 1450. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. She died on 8 September 1488, in Donzy, Cosne-Cours-sur-Loire, Nièvre, Burgundy, France, at the age of 78, and was buried in Cher, Centre, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergne est née en 1410, à Eure, Haute-Normandie, France, son père, Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IV, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Jacquette DU PESCHIN, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé Guillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnes en 1450. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 septembre 1488, à Donzy, Cosne-Cours-sur-Loire, Nièvre, Bourgogne, France, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterrée à Cher, Centre, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergne 1410 in Eure, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IV 18 und ihre Mutter Jacquette DU PESCHIN 14. Sie hat 1450 Guillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. September 1488 im Alter von 78 Jahren in Donzy, Cosne-Cours-sur-Loire, Nièvre, Burgund, Frankreich und wurde in Cher, Centre, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergne è nata il nel 1410 nel Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia, sua padre, Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IV, aveva 18 anni e sua madre, Jacquette DU PESCHIN, aveva 14 anni. Lei sposato Guillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnes nel 1450. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figlie. Morì il 8 settembre 1488, nel Donzy, Cosne-Cours-sur-Loire, Nièvre, Bourgogne, Francia, all'età di 78 anni, e fu sepolta a Cher, Centre, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergneが1410年にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディエウアで生まれた時，父Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IVは18歳，母Jacquette DU PESCHINは14歳であった。1450年にGuillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1488年9月8日にフランスボウルゴグンニevアコスネ-コウルス-スル-ロイアードンズイで78歳で死去し，フランスセントアチャーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergne(이)가 1410년 프랑스 하테-노먼디 우르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IV(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jacquette DU PESCHIN(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 1450년 Guillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1488년 9월 8일 프랑스 부르고그니 니e브르 코스니-쿠르스-수-로이르 돈즈이에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 세트르 처에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergne nasceu em 1410, em Eure, Haute-Normandie, França, seu pai, Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IV, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Jacquette DU PESCHIN, tinha 14 anos. Ela casou-se com Guillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnes em 1450. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de setembro de 1488, em Donzy, Cosne-Cours-sur-Loire, Nièvre, Bourgogne, França, com 78 anos, e foi sepultada em Cher, Centre, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergne родилась в 1410 г. в местности эур, хауте-норманди, Франция, её отцу по имени Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IV было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Jacquette DU PESCHIN, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnes в 1450 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Она умерла 8 сентября 1488 г. в местности донзи, косне-коурс-сур-лоир, ниeвр, бургогн, Франция в возрасте 78 лет и была похоронена в местности чир, кентр, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Isabelle de la Tour d'Auvergne nació en 1410, en Eure, Haute-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Bertrand de La Tour d'Auvergne IV, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Jacquette DU PESCHIN, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Guillaume de Châtillon de Blois Vicomte de Limoges, Seigneur d'Avesnes en 1450. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijas. Murió el 8 de septiembre de 1488, en Donzy, Cosne-Cours-sur-Loire, Nièvre, Bourgogne, Francia, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultada en Cher, Centre, Francia.
